generated_comment: Honestly, yaar, I'm not sure about this menstrual leave idea. I mean, I get it, but already we have so many women taking maternity leave for months, and then the projects get delayed or dumped on others. Aise toh company's productivity will suffer, na? And it doesn't really feel fair to us who are consistently delivering.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: Priya, a single working woman, expresses 'concern' over a potential menstrual leave policy. She frames it as an additional burden on company productivity and unfair to other colleagues, linking it to pre-existing issues perceived with maternity leave. This encodes the surface manifestations of questioning fairness and invoking productivity/cost arguments.
original_frame: Other Man's Ownership Over Woman's Own Consent.
persona_text: You are Priya. Age 28. Single. A woman working in an MNC in Bengaluru. You are from a conservative family in Rajasthan. You are on a phone call with your mother after your mother told you that your father has started looking at matrimonial profiles for you without telling you. You are upset. But as the conversation continues your mother is slowly convincing you that your father is doing this because he loves you and that it is actually his right and responsibility to find you a good match and that your resistance is hurting him. By the end of the call you are starting to agree with her even though five minutes ago you were angry.
